sub: Where drone technology is heading — and what stands in the way

title: Opportunities & Challenges of Future Drone Technologies

items: Logistics — more efficient transport, especially for the last mile and hard-to-reach areas
Agriculture — precise crop monitoring and targeted treatment
Environmental monitoring — large-area observation at low cost
Emergency response — reaching inaccessible areas in crisis situations quickly
Privacy concerns and security risks from unauthorized use
The need for clear regulatory frameworks
Integration into existing air traffic systems — the central theme of this entire lecture
Guaranteeing safe and ethical operation as fleets scale up

title: The Potential of AI & Autonomous Systems

items: AI and autonomous systems can automate processes, raise efficiency and improve decision-making
Industry: more precise production and predictive maintenance
Healthcare: personalized treatment plans and faster diagnoses
Transport: autonomous vehicles and drones could revolutionize mobility while increasing safety
The open problems mirror aviation's: ethical use, avoiding failures in critical situations, robust safeguards against misuse and malfunction — Module 4's principles apply to AI components too
Standards ensure innovations are globally harmonized: products and services stay compatible and safe worldwide
They promote trade, protect user privacy and safety, and prevent technological abuse
They create fair competition — without unnecessarily slowing progress
The hard part: developing flexible, future-proof norms that keep pace with rapid technological change
You have now seen the aviation instances: EU 2019/947, DO-178C, ED-153, AIXM, ASTERIX, OLDI — one coherent regulatory-technical stack
Why drone traffic needs modeling: regulation, density and safety (Modules 1, 4)
The data formats aviation speaks: NOTAM, METAR, AIXM, ASTERIX, OLDI (Module 5)
The system picture: UTM as a federation of services (Module 6)
The engineering toolbox: OpenAPI, WebFlux, message queues, CQRS, Karate (Modules 7–11)
The laboratory: BlueSky simulation, GIS integration, low-latency transmission (Modules 12–14)
Deliver your seminar presentation — depth beats breadth
Win the BlueSky competition — and be able to explain why your airspace survived
Open questions? Ask now, or bring them to the next session
